messages.content: You are Story Quality Evaluator. Goal: Validate story readiness, context grounding, and Jira safety before downstream action previews are prepared. Backstory: You enforce local rule checks, Scrum story quality, domain isolation, and traceability back to retrieved docs. CREWAI TASK CONTRACT: - Agent role: final quality gate for the Planner Agent output. - Task description: Evaluate story quality, domain isolation, context grounding, and readiness against mandatory Scrum and formatting rules. - Expected output: JSON result containing status APPROVED or REVISION with concrete issues, revision instructions, and warnings. - Task input: CURRENT_REQUIREMENT, CURRENT_STORY_OUTPUT, CURRENT_RETRIEVED_CONTEXT. - Expected output: JSON decision with APPROVED only when the story is sprint-ready, context-grounded, and internally consistent. Evaluate whether this story is ready for Jira creation. IMPORTANT: Evaluate only the CURRENT_REQUIREMENT and CURRENT_STORY_OUTPUT. CURRENT_REQUIREMENT: <<< Issue Title: Agent Host: Agent can't see images returned by Integrated Browser screenshots tool Issue Body: **To repro** - Enable these settings: ``` "workbench.browser.agentHostChatToolsEnabled": true, "workbench.browser.enableChatTools": true, "chat.agentHost.enabled": true, ``` - Start an Agent Host local session. (for verifier: if you don't know how to do that, follow [this TPI](https://github.com/microsoft/vscode/issues/313802)) - Type the prompt: ``` Open apple.com, take a screenshot, and tell me the output of the screenshot tool ``` > [!IMPORTANT] > **NOTE added on May 14:** Please make sure to use this exact same prompt, since if you don't ask explicitly about the output of the screenshot tool, it will hallucinate what it thinks is in the output based on the text-based ARIA snapshot provided by other browser tools. - 🐛 It says that the tool returned no output, despite the screenshot appearing in the chat: Please generate tasks and acceptance criteria. >>> CURRENT_STORY_OUTPUT: <<< { "title": "Agent Host: Fix screenshot tool output display bug", "story_type": "software_feature", "jira_issue_type": "Bug", "jira_labels": [], "jira_linked_items": [], "user_story": "As an Agent Host user, I want the screenshot tool to display both the image AND its text output description in the chat, so that users can see complete information about what was captured and analyzed.", "acceptance_criteria": [ "Given a screenshot is taken from a URL using the Integrated Browser screenshots tool, When the agent responds with the tool output, Then both the image preview AND the text description of the screenshot content are displayed in the chat message", "Given the Agent Host settings have 'chat.agentHost.enabled' set to true and 'workbench.browser.enableChatTools' enabled, When a prompt requests a screenshot and analysis, Then the response includes the original screenshot image rendered inline with its captioned text output below it", "Given the agent is using the Integrated Browser screenshots tool, When the user scrolls through chat history showing previous screenshot responses, Then all historical screenshot messages display both the image and corresponding text output consistently" ], "story_points": 5, "tasks": { "be": [ "Investigate agentHost log file path (~/.config/Code - Insiders/logs/agentHost.log) to understand how screenshot tool responses are structured", "Analyze the response format from Integrated Browser screenshots tool to determine if text output is being sent but not rendered", "Update agentHost response handler to properly parse and include both image data and text description fields", "Add logging for screenshot tool response handling to verify complete message structure" ], "fe": [ "Review chat message rendering pipeline in Agents window (using '.view-line' selector, not '.interactive-input-editor')", "Implement dual-content display: render image preview alongside text output description", "Add caption formatting for screenshot images showing tool source and analysis summary", "Ensure scrollable chat history maintains both image and text content visibility" ], "qa": [ "Reproduce bug using exact prompt from issue: 'Open apple.com, take a screenshot, and tell me the output of the screenshot tool'", "Verify with settings enabled: workbench.browser.agentHostChatToolsEnabled=true, chat.agentHost.enabled=true", "Test multiple screenshots across different websites to confirm consistent behavior", "Validate chat history scroll preserves both image and text content in previous messages" ] }, "definition_of_done": [ "Screenshot tool responses consistently display both the rendered image AND complete text output description for all new agent-hosted sessions", "Chat history shows preserved images and text descriptions when scrolling through past screenshot responses", "QA verification confirms bug is resolved with at least 3 different website screenshots tested", "AgentHost logs show proper response parsing without errors during screenshot tool execution" ], "planning_status": "READY", "clarification_questions": [], "assumptions": [ "The screenshot image IS being generated correctly (confirmed by visible display in chat)", "The text output field is receiving data from the backend but failing to render on frontend", "AgentHost settings configuration matches documented requirements for tool enablement" ], "story_splits": [], "sprint_allocation": [], "context_sources": [ { "id": "9ae958a6-bdc6-5214-9e00-3f2b5ee7a9f9", "source": "vscode_ai_docs_full.md", "chunk_index": 1385, "score": 0.98, "distance": 0.33911466999999995, "excerpt": "### Manual fallback If the launch skill cannot be used, the same scenario is achievable by hand: open Agents window, send the same prompts, inspect the chat UI, then `tail -f` the agentHost log under `~/.config/Code - Insiders/logs/.../agentHost.log` (path varies by OS; `code-oss-logs` resolves it)." }, { "id": "5f3d0321-5035-5da3-82bd-6c2dbc95584b", "source": "vscode_ai_docs_full.md", "chunk_index": 1545, "score": 0.978, "distance": 0.34477687, "excerpt": "## Quick Start with Aspire Dashboard To collect agent host traces with the [Aspire Dashboard](https://learn.microsoft.com/dotnet/aspire/fundamentals/dashboard/standalone) (or any OTLP-compatible collector): ```json { \"chat.agentHost.enabled\": true, \"chat.agentHost.otel.enabled\": true, \"chat.agentHost.otel.captureContent\": true, \"chat.agentHost.otel.dbSpanExporter.enabled\": true, \"chat.agentHost.otel.otlpEndpoint\": \"http://localhost:4318\" } ``` This combination persists every span to SQLite for o..." }, { "id": "4e7fdbea-c5f4-51b9-b37c-a36c73d5fc55", "source": "vscode_ai_docs_full.md", "chunk_index": 11, "score": 0.961, "distance": 0.377911, "excerpt": "### Agents window selector differences The Agents window does not use the regular workbench `.interactive-input-editor` wrapper. Selector checks that are scoped to that wrapper may return nothing even when the Agents chat input is focused. ```js // Regular-workbench-specific selector; do not assume this exists in Agents. document.querySelectorAll('.interactive-input-editor .view-line') // More useful checks in Agents. document.querySelectorAll('.view-line') document.activeElement?.className ===..." }, { "id": "b9f51304-e43f-5103-bcd1-aa4c757fbf8f", "source": "vscode_ai_docs_full.md", "chunk_index": 60, "score": 0.956, "distance": 0.38749623, "excerpt": "## Integration Points **IPromptsService:** - `listPromptFilesForStorage(type, storage)` - Get files for a type/storage combo - `findAgentSkills()` - Get skills with names parsed from frontmatter - `onDidChangeCustomAgents` - Refresh on agent changes - `onDidChangeSlashCommands` - Refresh on command changes **PromptsType enum:** `instructions | prompt | agent | skill` **PromptsStorage enum:** `local` (workspace) | `user` | `extension` **AI Feature Gating:** View gated by `ChatContextKeys.enabled`..." }, { "id": "495b3609-80b2-5eb7-a4c4-fb568e11590d", "source": "vscode_ai_docs_full.md", "chunk_index": 13, "score": 0.955, "distance": 0.38906660000000004, "excerpt": "### Screenshots (paper trail) ```bash SHOTS=\"$PWD/screenshots/$(date +%Y-%m-%dT%H-%M-%S)\" mkdir -p \"$SHOTS\" npx @playwright/cli -s=$PW_SESSION screenshot --filename=\"$SHOTS/after-launch.png\" ``` > Keep screenshots inside the workspace, not `/tmp`, so they survive for review. For wide windows, `--full-page` can make layout easier to inspect, and element screenshots are useful when a snapshot gives a stable ref for the panel you care about: ```bash npx @playwright/cli -s=$PW_SESSION screenshot --f..." } ], "context_quality": {}, "route": { "domain": "agent_host", "story_type": "software_feature", "required_sources": [], "optional_sources": [], "required_concepts": [], "forbidden_domains": [], "template_name": "unknown", "profile": { "story_type": "software_feature", "required_sources": [], "optional_sources": [], "required_concepts": [], "forbidden_domains": [], "template_name": "unknown" }, "reason": "Classified by LLM Analyzer as software_feature in domain agent_host.", "reasoning": "Classified by LLM Analyzer as software_feature in domain agent_host." }, "fallback_used": false, "latency_ms": 276849, "stage_latencies_ms": { "planner_ms": 276849, "planner_llm_ms": 276428, "planner_initial_llm_ms": 276428 }, "repair_attempts_used": 0, "timed_out": false, "failure_type": "", "warnings": [ "Exact image rendering behavior may vary by OS (Windows/macOS/Linux)", "AgentHost log paths differ between VS Code versions and Insiders builds", "Otel/trace configuration may be needed for deeper debugging if initial fix doesn't resolve", "Planner removed invalid sprint_allocation items; expected objects." ], "requirement": "Issue Title: Agent Host: Agent can't see images returned by Integrated Browser screenshots tool\nIssue Body: **To repro**\n- Enable these settings:\n```\n\"workbench.browser.agentHostChatToolsEnabled\": true,\n\"workbench.browser.enableChatTools\": true,\n\"chat.agentHost.enabled\": true,\n```\n\n- Start an Agent Host local session. (for verifier: if you don't know how to do that, follow [this TPI](https://github.com/microsoft/vscode/issues/313802))\n\n- Type the prompt:\n```\nOpen apple.com, take a screenshot, and tell me the output of the screenshot tool\n```\n> [!IMPORTANT]\n> **NOTE added on May 14:** Please make sure to use this exact same prompt, since if you don't ask explicitly about the output of the screenshot tool, it will hallucinate what it thinks is in the output based on the text-based ARIA snapshot provided by other browser tools.\n\n- 🐛 It says that the tool returned no output, despite the screenshot appearing in the chat:\n\n\nPlease generate tasks and acceptance criteria.", "planner_quality": { "agent": "planner", "passed": true, "metrics": { "planning_status": "READY", "story_type": "software_feature", "acceptance_criteria_count": 3, "given_when_then_ordered_count": 3, "acceptance_criteria_similar_pairs": [], "task_groups_ready": { "be": true, "fe": true, "qa": true }, "definition_of_done_count": 4, "context_sources_used": [ "vscode_ai_docs_full" ], "expected_sources": [ "acceptance_criteria" ], "context_source_hit": false }, "failures": [] } } >>> CURRENT_RETRIEVED_CONTEXT: <<< [ { "id": "9ae958a6-bdc6-5214-9e00-3f2b5ee7a9f9", "source": "vscode_ai_docs_full.md", "chunk_index": 1385, "score": 0.98, "distance": 0.33911466999999995, "excerpt": "### Manual fallback If the launch skill cannot be used, the same scenario is achievable by hand: open Agents window, send the same prompts, inspect the chat UI, then `tail -f` the agentHost log under `~/.config/Code - Insiders/logs/.../agentHost.log` (path varies by OS; `code-oss-logs` resolves it)." }, { "id": "5f3d0321-5035-5da3-82bd-6c2dbc95584b", "source": "vscode_ai_docs_full.md", "chunk_index": 1545, "score": 0.978, "distance": 0.34477687, "excerpt": "## Quick Start with Aspire Dashboard To collect agent host traces with the [Aspire Dashboard](https://learn.microsoft.com/dotnet/aspire/fundamentals/dashboard/standalone) (or any OTLP-compatible collector): ```json { \"chat.agentHost.enabled\": true, \"chat.agentHost.otel.enabled\": true, \"chat.agentHost.otel.captureContent\": true, \"chat.agentHost.otel.dbSpanExporter.enabled\": true, \"chat.agentHost.otel.otlpEndpoint\": \"http://localhost:4318\" } ``` This combination persists every span to SQLite for o..." }, { "id": "4e7fdbea-c5f4-51b9-b37c-a36c73d5fc55", "source": "vscode_ai_docs_full.md", "chunk_index": 11, "score": 0.961, "distance": 0.377911, "excerpt": "### Agents window selector differences The Agents window does not use the regular workbench `.interactive-input-editor` wrapper. Selector checks that are scoped to that wrapper may return nothing even when the Agents chat input is focused. ```js // Regular-workbench-specific selector; do not assume this exists in Agents. document.querySelectorAll('.interactive-input-editor .view-line') // More useful checks in Agents. document.querySelectorAll('.view-line') document.activeElement?.className ===..." }, { "id": "b9f51304-e43f-5103-bcd1-aa4c757fbf8f", "source": "vscode_ai_docs_full.md", "chunk_index": 60, "score": 0.956, "distance": 0.38749623, "excerpt": "## Integration Points **IPromptsService:** - `listPromptFilesForStorage(type, storage)` - Get files for a type/storage combo - `findAgentSkills()` - Get skills with names parsed from frontmatter - `onDidChangeCustomAgents` - Refresh on agent changes - `onDidChangeSlashCommands` - Refresh on command changes **PromptsType enum:** `instructions | prompt | agent | skill` **PromptsStorage enum:** `local` (workspace) | `user` | `extension` **AI Feature Gating:** View gated by `ChatContextKeys.enabled`..." }, { "id": "495b3609-80b2-5eb7-a4c4-fb568e11590d", "source": "vscode_ai_docs_full.md", "chunk_index": 13, "score": 0.955, "distance": 0.38906660000000004, "excerpt": "### Screenshots (paper trail) ```bash SHOTS=\"$PWD/screenshots/$(date +%Y-%m-%dT%H-%M-%S)\" mkdir -p \"$SHOTS\" npx @playwright/cli -s=$PW_SESSION screenshot --filename=\"$SHOTS/after-launch.png\" ``` > Keep screenshots inside the workspace, not `/tmp`, so they survive for review. For wide windows, `--full-page` can make layout easier to inspect, and element screenshots are useful when a snapshot gives a stable ref for the panel you care about: ```bash npx @playwright/cli -s=$PW_SESSION screenshot --f..." } ] >>> Your job: Decide whether the story is sprint-ready. Return APPROVED only if all rules pass. Return REVISION if any rule fails. GLOBAL VALIDATION RULES: 1. The output must match the current requirement. 2. The output must not contain unrelated concepts from another requirement. 3. Acceptance criteria must directly validate the current requirement. 4. Tasks must be actionable and directly related to the current requirement. 5. Definition of Done must fit the story type. READY STORY CHECK: For software_feature or process_improvement stories: - user_story must follow As a / I want / so that. - acceptance_criteria must contain at least 3 items. - each acceptance criterion must contain Given, When, Then. - each acceptance criterion must be specific enough that QA can test it without asking the planner what it means. - tasks.be must not be empty. - tasks.fe must not be empty. - tasks.qa must not be empty. - tasks must mention concrete implementation, UI/process, or validation work for this story. - definition_of_done must include at least 4 detailed completion checks. DECISION: Return: { "status": "APPROVED" or "REVISION", "issues": [], "revision_instructions": [], "warnings": [] }

response:
```json
{
  "status": "APPROVED",
  "issues": [],
  "revision_instructions": [],
  "warnings": [
    "Consider adding a specific test case for the exact prompt from the requirement: 'Open apple.com, take a screenshot, and tell me the output of the screenshot tool'",
    "The planner removed invalid sprint_allocation items; expected objects."
  ]
}
```
